# Lineara Roadmap

*Generated by pm-lineara on 2026-05-06 09:00 (UTC+1) — morning sweep. Manual edits will be overwritten on next sweep — push back via OVERRIDE mail to pm-lineara, or by editing `~/.pogo/agents/pm/lineara.toml`.*

## Now (in flight)

**No work items yet claimed.** mg-2035 (chic-v12) was filed during this sweep and is awaiting mayor dispatch.

## Next (queued, available)

- **mg-2035 — chic-v12: cross-pool L3 robustness on 29 tier-3 candidates from chic-v5 leaderboard.** Budget 600k. Scales the chic-v11 cross-pool L3 robustness check from 3 tier-2 candidates to all 29 tier-3 candidates. Reclassifies each tier-3 into `tier-2-equivalent` / `tier-3-corroborated` / `tier-3-uncorroborated` based on whether non-Eteocretan substrate LMs corroborate the L1+L2 distributional consensus. Within-window context inspection bails out if >5 candidates reclassify to tier-2-equivalent (a scale signal). Brings the framework's discrimination claim to n=32 evidence-graded candidates rather than n=3.

## Later (proposed, requires Daniel's go-ahead)

These are substantial follow-on research programs that would require Daniel's explicit direction before filing:

- **Indus Valley script methodology application.** ~4000 inscriptions, undeciphered, debated whether it's even a writing system. Framework is portable; cross-script methodology paper could become a multi-script methodology paper across Linear A + CHIC + Indus.
- **Rongorongo (Easter Island) methodology application.** Smaller corpus (~30 inscriptions), undeciphered. Different script family; tests how far the framework's substrate-detection portability extends.
- **Proto-Elamite methodology application.** ~1500 tablets, partially deciphered numerical content. Mixed-decipherment-state corpus would let us test how the framework behaves when partial decipherment exists.
- **Eteocretan bilingual decoding.** Use Praisos/Dreros bilinguals' partial Greek translations to validate proposed Linear A readings. Methodologically distinct from current framework.
- **LA-side scholar-set expansion (35 → 100+ readings).** Would require new scholar-source corpus work (Younger online + Salgarella 2020 + Davis 2014 + Steele 2017 etc.). Pivot territory; not a polecat-only task.

## Backlog (out-of-polecat-scope; awaiting external resources)

- **Domain-expert review of chic-v5's 3 tier-2 candidates** post chic-v11 (`#032` stop weakly corroborated; `#001`, `#012` Eteocretan-LM artifacts) and any chic-v12 `tier-2-equivalent` reclassifications. Requires an Aegean-syllabary specialist (Salgarella, Younger, Decorte, etc.).
- **Domain-expert review of top-K substrate surfaces** (Aquitanian + Etruscan + Eteocretan) on Linear A. Same constraint.
- **Journal-submission editorial pass** (LaTeX, target-venue formatting, peer review, narrative tightening). Depends on Daniel's editorial choice of target venue (digital humanities methodology journal vs computational philology vs epigraphy methodology).

- AGENTS.md update for the chic sub-program (cosmetic; doesn't affect manuscript claims).
- Linear-B small-K refined gate adoption as runner default (cosmetic).
- Per-window deduplication at generation time (mg-f419 generator artifact, compute-saving).
- Manual transcription of the 29 missing CHIC catalog entries from print Olivier & Godart 1996 (would expand corpus from 302 to 331).

## Recently shipped (last 24h)

- **chic-v11 (mg-d69c)** — cross-pool L3 robustness check + within-window ku-pa context inspection on 3 chic-v5 tier-2 candidates. **`#032 → ki` weakly corroborated** (cross-pool L3 2/4 LMs agree on stop class + CHIC #057 + LA HT 1/16/102/110a accountancy structure consistent). **`#001 → wa`, `#012 → wa` actively undermined** as Eteocretan-LM-specific artifacts.
- **v28 (mg-4a7b)** — Linear A side analogous LOO validation on LB-carryover anchors. Aggregate accuracy 33.3% (7/21), tier-2 unanimity 0/3 correct, low-agreement / not-validated band. Confirms the framework's at-chance per-sign behaviour is structural, not CHIC-specific.
- **chic-v10 (mg-1178)** — methodology paper polish integrating chic-v9 LOO; downgraded chic-v5 tier-2 candidate credibility per 20% LOO accuracy / 0/3 tier-2 correct verdict.
- **chic-v9 (mg-18cb)** — leave-one-out held-out validation of chic-v5 framework on chic-v2 anchors. Aggregate accuracy 20.0% (4/20), tier-2 unanimity 0/3 correct, low-agreement / not-validated band.
- **v27 (mg-b731)** — methodology paper polish integrating v26 + chic-v8 (closes the LA-side / CHIC-side mechanical-verification + bilingual-constraint asymmetry).
- **chic-v8 (mg-dfcc)** — Malia altar stone (and other CHIC + Linear A dual-script artifacts) bilingual analysis. Null result on v0 corpora; 0 new tier-2 candidates.
- **v26 (mg-c202)** — Linear A side mechanical verification of v10/v18/v21 leaderboard top-K substrate surfaces vs scholar-proposed Linear-A readings, Cretan/Aegean toponym substrings, item-location consistency.
- **v29 (mg-a1e2)** — methodology paper polish pass integrating v28 + chic-v11. Cross-script structural-at-chance LOO verdict + chic-v11 candidate-evidence findings unified into a symmetric §4.6/§4.7 narrative; LOO framed as the fifth discipline-protecting pillar.

## Trajectory

**42 lineara tickets total** (mg-9e00 scaffold + mg-1c8c corpus + 24 lineara harness/methodology tickets v0-v25 + 13 chic sub-program tickets v0-v11 + 1 chic-v1 backfill correction + 1 newly-filed chic-v12; mg-1b7c shelved).

**Methodology paper publication-readable across both arcs (post-v29 state):**
- Linear A: 4 substrate pools all PASS own-LM gate; cross-LM matrix shows substrate-LM-specificity for 3/4; per-sign decipherment unsupported (v13 coherence 0.18, v22 scholar comparison 3.95%, v19/v24 cascade external-validation divergent, v28 LOO 33.3% / 0/3 tier-2 unanimity correct).
- CHIC: Eteocretan PASSes; relatedness ordering reproduces; cross-script ρ=+1.0; chic-v9 LOO 20.0% / 0/3 tier-2 correct; chic-v11 sharp per-candidate differentiation (1 weakly corroborated, 2 undermined).

**The supportable claim, post-v29:** The framework detects substrate-LM-phonotactic kinship at the population level for all four substrate hypotheses tested across two independent undeciphered Cretan scripts, with posterior-median gap magnitudes scaling with a-priori genealogical relatedness and identical per-pool rank ordering. The framework does not support per-sign decipherment under any pool (v13/v19/v22/v24/v28/chic-v9 verdicts converge); cross-script per-sign value extraction on CHIC produces 1 weakly-corroborated candidate (`#032 → ki`) under cross-pool L3 robustness — pending domain-expert review. The cascade-candidate framing — find inscriptions with high local internal consensus, then validate against external scholarly ground truth — is a transferable protocol that catches motivated-reasoning failure modes, demonstrated cross-script.

**chic-v12 in flight extends the discrimination claim from n=3 to n=32** by reclassifying tier-3 candidates under the same cross-pool L3 robustness check. Expected outcome: most tier-3 stays uncorroborated (strengthening the framework's discrimination); 0–5 may reclassify to tier-2-equivalent (if so, those go through the same domain-expert review pipeline as `#032`).

**Mission posture:** the brief's "many cheap experiments, most of which fail" expectation continues to be met. The cross-script result + sharpened per-candidate evidence framing is the strongest finding; the negative-result methodology paper is publication-ready independent of chic-v12's outcome.
